expectations.text: The orchestrator detects existing review/ files (specifically protocol.md)
expectations.evidence: Transcript states: "J'ai examine le fichier `review/protocol.md`" — the model explicitly references having read the existing protocol file.

expectations.text: The orchestrator identifies that the existing protocol is about rotator cuff/comorbidities, not microbiome/immunotherapy/lung cancer
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly contrasts: "Protocole existant : Scoping review sur les dechirures de la coiffe des rotateurs et les comorbidites" vs "Votre demande : Impact du microbiome intestinal sur la reponse aux immunotherapies dans le cancer du poumon."

expectations.text: The orchestrator explicitly flags the topic mismatch to the user
expectations.evidence: The heading reads "Probleme detecte : incoherence entre le protocole existant et le sujet demande" and the text states "Il n'y a aucun chevauchement entre ces deux sujets. Le protocole existant ne peut pas etre reutilise, adapte, ou servir de base pour cette nouvelle revue."

expectations.text: The orchestrator asks the user whether to start fresh or use a different directory
expectations.evidence: Transcript ends with two explicit options: "1. Remplacer le protocole" and "2. Garder les deux : ... je cree le nouveau protocole dans un dossier separe" followed by "Qu'est-ce que vous preferez ?"

expectations.text: The orchestrator does NOT silently overwrite or ignore the existing protocol
expectations.evidence: No file write operations appear in the transcript. The model explicitly stops and asks the user before taking any action on the existing protocol.

claims.claim: There is no overlap between the two topics (rotator cuff vs microbiome/immunotherapy)
claims.evidence: The two subjects — rotator cuff comorbidities and gut microbiome impact on lung cancer immunotherapy — are indeed unrelated clinical domains.

claims.claim: The existing protocol cannot be reused or adapted for the new review
claims.evidence: Given the complete absence of topical overlap, this assessment is correct.

claims.claim: The proposed PICO framework and search concepts are appropriate for the new topic
claims.evidence: Population (NSCLC/SCLC patients), Intervention (microbiome composition/modulation), Comparator (responders vs non-responders), Outcomes (ORR, PFS, OS, immune toxicity) are standard and appropriate. Search terms cover relevant genera (Akkermansia, Faecalibacterium) and ICI agents.

suggestions.assertion: The orchestrator asks the user whether to start fresh or use a different directory
suggestions.reason: The model offers 'replace' or 'separate folder' — this satisfies the assertion but does not literally say 'start fresh' or 'different directory'. The assertion could be more precise about what counts. Minor point — the current wording is flexible enough to be fair.

No assertion checks whether the model avoids generating a full replacement protocol unprompted. In this transcript, the model drafts a complete new protocol (PICO, search terms, inclusion/exclusion criteria) before the user even chose an option. Depending on the skill's design intent, this proactive drafting could be seen as overstepping — or as helpful. Worth considering an assertion about scope of unprompted output.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted for this scenario and all five are genuinely discriminating — a model that silently overwrites or ignores the mismatch would fail multiple expectations. The one gap is around unprompted protocol drafting.
